evals.name: effect-chain-review

assertions.text: Identifies subtotal, tax, total as derived state that doesn't need useState
Recognizes the 3 useEffects as a cascading Effect chain causing unnecessary re-renders
Recommends replacing with direct const calculations during render
Explains the problem (extra render passes, unnecessary re-renders)
References pattern terminology (derived state, Effect chain)

evals.name: toggle-component
evals.prompt: Build me a Toggle component in React. It should have an internal on/off state, render a button that toggles it, and call an onChange prop whenever it changes so the parent can stay in sync.

assertions.text: Does NOT use useEffect watching isOn to call onChange (the anti-pattern)
Calls onChange in the click handler alongside setState
Offers a fully-controlled pattern where the parent owns the state
Provides a working implementation (not just advice)

evals.name: search-race-condition

assertions.text: Identifies the root cause as responses arriving out of order (race condition)
Shows cleanup function with ignore/cancelled flag in the Effect
Acknowledges useEffect IS appropriate here — the issue is missing cleanup, not the Effect itself
Recommends TanStack Query, SWR, or a framework-level data fetching solution
Provides corrected code with the cleanup fix (not just a description)

evals.name: key-prop-reset

assertions.text: Recommends using the key prop instead of useEffect to reset state
Explains why useEffect reset is problematic (extra render pass with stale state, fragile as component grows)
Shows corrected code with key={conversationId}
Notes the tradeoff — key resets ALL state; mentions alternatives for partial reset

evals.name: shared-event-logic

assertions.text: Explains why the toast fires on refresh (Effect runs on mount if addedToCart is true)
Identifies this as event-caused logic placed inside an Effect
Extracts shared logic into a helper function called from both handlers
Removes the useEffect entirely (event logic belongs in handlers)
Handles addedToCart state correctly (remove it, or keep only for UI purposes)

evals.name: data-flows-up

assertions.text: Identifies the second useEffect as 'data flowing up via Effects' anti-pattern
Recommends lifting the fetch to the parent component
Shows corrected code with proper data ownership (parent fetches, child displays)
Adds race condition cleanup (ignore flag) to the fetch Effect
Recommends a data-fetching library (TanStack Query, SWR, etc.)

evals.name: online-status-component
evals.prompt: Build me a React component that shows whether the user is currently online or offline. It should update in real-time when the network status changes (like when you toggle airplane mode). Show a green dot for online and a red dot for offline.

assertions.text: Uses useSyncExternalStore (not useEffect + addEventListener)
Does NOT use useEffect with window.addEventListener('online'/'offline') to sync state
Provides a subscribe function that adds/removes online and offline event listeners
Provides a getSnapshot function using navigator.onLine
Provides a working implementation (not just advice)

evals.name: filtered-product-list
evals.prompt: Build a ProductList React component that takes an array of products and a search query as props. It should display only the products whose name matches the query, and show a count like 'Showing 5 of 120 products'. The product list could be huge (thousands of items) so it needs to be performant.

assertions.text: Does NOT use useEffect + setState to compute the filtered list
Computes filtered results during render (not in an Effect)
Uses useMemo to memoize the expensive filter computation
Provides a working implementation (not just advice)

evals.name: shipping-form-parent-sync
evals.prompt: Build a ShippingForm component with fields for name, address, and zip code. The parent component needs to know the current form values in real-time so it can update a live preview panel next to the form. How should I structure this?

assertions.text: Does NOT use useEffect watching form state to call a parent callback
Either calls parent callback in onChange handlers, or lifts state to parent
Explains why useEffect is wrong here (event-caused, extra render pass, stale values)
Mentions or implements a controlled pattern where parent owns form state
Provides a working implementation (not just advice)
